context.summary: Use the micro:bitâs radio feature to scare your friends at Halloween.
how_it_works: There is one program, which is added to both micro:bits.
The program sets the radio group to 31. Groups are like channels, so any micro:bit using the same group will get the message. You can use any group number you like from 0-255.
You can send one of three scary messages. Press button A to send the message âangryâ, press button B to send the message âskullâ and press the touch logo to send the message âghostâ.
The micro:bit can send messages as numbers or text. You're using text messages in this project, so make sure you use radio blocks that refer toÂ stringsÂ rather thanÂ numbersÂ orÂ values.
In computing, aÂ stringÂ is a sequence of characters that can contain letters, numbers, symbols, and spaces.
When the other micro:bit receives the radio message, it shows either an angry face, a skull or ghost on its LED display, depending on the message received, and different scary sounds play.
The relevant icon is also shown on the display of your micro:bit so that you can see the message has been sent.
improve_it: Design your own scary icons with the 'show LEDs' block.
Add animations instead of static icons.
Compose some creepy music that plays when the second micro:bit receives a message.
